content.full_text:
State of California 
Memorandum 
To 
Opinion Request Participants 
Date 
3/26/81 
From 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
Lynn 
Subject: 
TA & A Question (from Helen) at Opinion Request meeting of 
March 19, 1981. 
Q. 
An elected city official's controlled committee makes 
an independent expenditure of more than $500 for a 
mailing which benefits a slate of candidates, but does 
not spend $500 or more, allocated on a pro-rata basis, 
on anyone candidate. 
What statements must be filed, 
and where? 
A. 
Section 84200(b) provides: In addition to the campaign 
statements required by subdivision (a), candidates, 
elected officers, their controlled committees, and 
any committee formed primarily to support or oppose a 
candidate or a measure that has made or received contri-
butions of five hundred dollars ($500) or more or made 
expenditures of five hundred dollars ($500) or more 
during the threshold period ... shall file campaign 
statements .... Therefore, a threshold statement is 
required and is filed where the maker of the independent 
expenditure normally files campaign statements. 
Section 84208(a) provides: In addition to any campaign 
statements required by Section 84200, if a committee 
has made independent expenditures totaling five hundred 
dollars ($500) or more in a calendar year to support 
or oppose a candidate, a measure or qualification of 
a measure It shall file independent expenditure reports ... 
The elected official has not spent $500 or more on any 
one candidate, allocated on a pro-rata basis, and, there-
fore, is not required to file an independent expenditure 
report.

sections.question: An elected city official's controlled committee makes an independent expenditure of more than $500 for a mailing which benefits a slate of candidates, but does not spend $500 or more, allocated on a pro-rata basis, on anyone candidate. What statements must be filed, and where?
sections.conclusion: Section 84200(b) provides: In addition to the campaign statements required by subdivision (a), candidates, elected officers, their controlled committees, and any committee formed primarily to support or oppose a candidate or a measure that has made or received contributions of five hundred dollars ($500) or more or made expenditures of five hundred dollars ($500) or more during the threshold period ... shall file campaign statements .... Therefore, a threshold statement is required and is filed where the maker of the independent expenditure normally files campaign statements. Section 84208(a) provides: In addition to any campaign statements required by Section 84200, if a committee has made independent expenditures totaling five hundred dollars ($500) or more in a calendar year to support or oppose a candidate, a measure or qualification of a measure it shall file independent expenditure reports ... The elected official has not spent $500 or more on any one candidate, allocated on a pro-rata basis, and, therefore, is not required to file an independent expenditure report.
sections.question_synthetic: When an elected official's controlled committee makes independent expenditures exceeding $500 for a mailing benefiting multiple candidates without $500+ allocated to any single candidate, what filing requirements apply and where must statements be filed?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: A threshold campaign statement is required and must be filed where the elected official normally files campaign statements due to the $500+ expenditure threshold. However, no independent expenditure report is required since the expenditure does not allocate $500 or more to any single candidate.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is a memorandum from FPPC responding to a question raised at an opinion request meeting. Contains clear Q and A sections. Dated March 26, 1981, Document ID 81A146.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: An elected city official's controlled committee makes an independent expenditure of more than $500 for a mailing which benefits a slate of candidates, but does not spend $500 or more, allocated on a pro-rata basis, on anyone candidate. What statements must be filed, and where?

CONCLUSION: Section 84200(b) provides: In addition to the campaign statements required by subdivision (a), candidates, elected officers, their controlled committees, and any committee formed primarily to support or oppose a candidate or a measure that has made or received contributions of five hundred dollars ($500) or more or made expenditures of five hundred dollars ($500) or more during the threshold period ... shall file campaign statements .... Therefore, a threshold statement is required and is filed where the maker of the independent expenditure normally files campaign statements. Section 84208(a) provides: In addition to any campaign statements required by Section 84200, if a committee has made independent expenditures totaling five hundred dollars ($500) or more in a calendar year to support or oppose a candidate, a measure or qualification of a measure it shall file independent expenditure reports ... The elected official has not spent $500 or more on any one candidate, allocated on a pro-rata basis, and, therefore, is not required to file an independent expenditure report.
embedding.first_500_words: State of California Memorandum To Opinion Request Participants Date 3/26/81 From FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION Lynn Subject: TA & A Question (from Helen) at Opinion Request meeting of March 19, 1981. Q. An elected city official's controlled committee makes an independent expenditure of more than $500 for a mailing which benefits a slate of candidates, but does not spend $500 or more, allocated on a pro-rata basis, on anyone candidate. What statements must be filed, and where? A. Section 84200(b) provides: In addition to the campaign statements required by subdivision (a), candidates, elected officers, their controlled committees, and any committee formed primarily to support or oppose a candidate or a measure that has made or received contri- butions of five hundred dollars ($500) or more or made expenditures of five hundred dollars ($500) or more during the threshold period ... shall file campaign statements .... Therefore, a threshold statement is required and is filed where the maker of the independent expenditure normally files campaign statements. Section 84208(a) provides: In addition to any campaign statements required by Section 84200, if a committee has made independent expenditures totaling five hundred dollars ($500) or more in a calendar year to support or oppose a candidate, a measure or qualification of a measure It shall file independent expenditure reports ... The elected official has not spent $500 or more on any one candidate, allocated on a pro-rata basis, and, there- fore, is not required to file an independent expenditure report.
embedding.summary: FPPC advises that an elected official's controlled committee making independent expenditures over $500 for a multi-candidate mailing must file a threshold campaign statement but not an independent expenditure report, since the pro-rata allocation to individual candidates does not reach $500.
